description_long: This restaurant features a traditional Japanese architectural design with a prominent tiled gabled roof, wooden beams, and sliding shoji-style doors and windows. The structure is elevated on a wooden platform with steps leading up to the entrance, and lanterns hang from the eaves, adding a warm, inviting ambiance. The interior is visible from multiple sides, revealing tatami mat flooring, low dining tables, and decorative screens, all arranged in an open-plan layout. The exterior walls are a mix of dark wood and light plaster, with latticework and bamboo accents. Several human figures and lanterns are positioned around the entrance, suggesting a lively, welcoming atmosphere. The overall design emphasizes symmetry, natural materials, and a harmonious blend with the environment.
description: A traditional Japanese restaurant with a gabled tiled roof, wooden structure, lanterns, and open tatami-mat interior visible from multiple sides.
description_view_0: Side view showing the main staircase, lanterns, and a clear look at the elevated wooden platform and open dining area.
description_view_1: Frontal view highlighting the main entrance, symmetrical facade, interior dining setup, and decorative screens.
description_view_2: Side profile emphasizing the length of the building, tiled roof, and shoji-style windows with minimal ornamentation.
description_view_3: Rear view displaying the back entrance, roof structure, and a glimpse of the interior layout.
attribute_facade_design: The facade features dark wooden beams, white plaster infill, and a prominent tiled gabled roof with decorative ridge tiles. Hanging lanterns and bamboo accents add traditional Japanese charm, while the entrance is marked by wide steps and a wooden railing. The overall style is symmetrical and understated, emphasizing natural materials and clean lines.
